---
title: "Data Preparation"
---

## Overview

This section documents the ETL pipeline used to extract, clean, and structure the raw Lightcast job postings dataset for analysis.

## ETL Pipeline

The pipeline runs in three steps via `etl/etl_pipeline.ipynb`:

| Step | Notebook | Module | Output |
|------|----------|--------|--------|
| Extract | 01_extract.ipynb | extract.py | Raw DataFrame loaded into memory |
| Transform | 02_transform.ipynb | transform.py | Cleaned, column-reduced DataFrame |
| Load | 03_load.ipynb | load.py | Relational CSVs saved to data/processed/ |

## Transform Decisions

- **Columns dropped:** Metadata fields (URLs, timestamps), redundant code columns where NAME versions were kept, sparse skill/certification columns
- **Missing value strategies:** Salary filled with median; education/employment type filled with Unknown; experience left as-is (null = not specified)
- **Red/Blue state join:** POLITICAL_LEAN column ready to merge once state_political_lean.csv is supplied

## Processed Tables

| File | Rows | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| lightcast_job_postings_relational.csv | 72,455 | Core fact table |
| lightcast_salary.csv | 8,092 | Salary range by job |
| lightcast_location.csv | 52 | State lookup |
| lightcast_industry.csv | 22 | NAICS2 industry labels |
| lightcast_education.csv | 7 | Education level labels |
| lightcast_experience.csv | 18 | Experience range labels |
| lightcast_occupation.csv | 2 | SOC occupation labels |
| household.csv | 1,348,324 | IPUMS household data |
